commonIssues:
The MeeSeva portal is often reported as unresponsive or slow during working hours.

How to fix:

  • Try accessing the MeeSeva Portal during non-peak hours (early morning) and ensure documents are compressed to the required size.

Missing affidavits, discrepancies in age proof (e.g., Aadhaar vs. SSC certificate), or mismatched addresses can lead to immediate rejection.

How to fix:

  • Cross-verify Aadhaar and SSC certificates. If mismatched, update Aadhaar details via the UIDAI portal first.

Couples often struggle to determine whether to apply under the Hindu Marriage Act or the Special Marriage Act, which have different requirements.

How to fix:

  • Ensure both applicants follow the correct act (Hindu Marriage Act for same-religion, Special Marriage Act for inter-religion).

Last-minute issues with finding suitable witnesses or getting witnesses to appear physically for the registration process.

How to fix:

  • Keep 3 witnesses ready with original ID proofs. Witnesses must be present physically at the SRO on the appointment day.

Women married across state borders often face issues deleting their names from their native ration cards, which is required for registration.

How to fix:

  • Obtain a surrender certificate from your native ration card office and delete your name online via the AP EPDS Portal.

Mistakes in spelling names of the bride, groom, or their parents, which can cause significant issues later.

How to fix:

  • Double-check spellings. For corrections, submit an affidavit to the SRO.

Even after online submission, a physical visit to the Sub-Registrar’s Office (SRO) is necessary for verification and signatures, which can be time-consuming.

How to fix:

  • Visit the AP Registration Department physical office promptly after online approval with all original documents.

Complaints indicate that, despite online processes, unofficial demands for payments (bribes) to expedite the process by staff are common.

How to fix:

  • Insist on online fee receipts. Report unofficial demands via the AP Anti-Corruption Bureau helpline.

Incorrect spelling of names or dates on the final certificate, rendering it useless for passport, visa, or banking purposes.

How to fix:

  • Apply for a correction immediately at the MeeSeva center with a notarized affidavit.

Failure to update official documents in the correct order (Aadhaar PAN Passport) can create a legal deadlock.

How to fix:

  • Update documents sequentially: Aadhaar -> PAN -> Passport.

Failing to register within 30-90 days can lead to additional fees and complicated processes, particularly for old marriages.

How to fix:

  • Register within 30 days. If delayed, pay the required late fee online at MeeSeva.
